📖  Grammar — Past Continuous — was/were + -ing (background action in a story)
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

The PAST CONTINUOUS describes a BACKGROUND ACTION or ONGOING SITUATION at a specific time in the past. Form: WAS/WERE + verb-ING. It is often used with PAST SIMPLE to show: 1) A longer background action interrupted by a shorter one: 'I WAS SLEEPING (continuous) when the phone RANG (past simple).' 2) Two parallel actions happening at the same time: 'While she WAS COOKING, he WAS WATCHING TV.' Signal words: when, while, as, at that moment, at 7pm, all morning.

📖  Grammar — Past Simple vs Past Continuous — when and while
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

KEY RULE: PAST CONTINUOUS + WHEN + PAST SIMPLE = a longer action interrupted by a shorter one. 'I WAS SLEEPING (long) WHEN the phone RANG (short, interrupted).' PAST SIMPLE + WHILE + PAST CONTINUOUS = the same idea from a different angle. 'The phone RANG (simple) WHILE I WAS SLEEPING (continuous).' Both mean the same! WHEN + Past Simple = two completed actions in sequence: 'WHEN she arrived, I LEFT.' (first she arrived, then I left).

ГОЛ ДҮРЭМ: PAST CONTINUOUS + WHEN + PAST SIMPLE = урт үйлдлийг богино үйлдэл таслана. 'I WAS SLEEPING WHEN the phone RANG.' PAST SIMPLE + WHILE + PAST CONTINUOUS = ижил санаа өөр өнцгөөс. 'The phone RANG WHILE I WAS SLEEPING.' Хоёул ижил утгатай! WHEN + Past Simple + Past Simple = дараалсан хоёр дууссан үйлдэл: 'WHEN she arrived, I LEFT.' (эхлээд тэр ирсэн, дараа нь би явсан).

It rang while I was sleeping.
It didn't ring while I was sleeping.

When she left, I didn't go.

What happened as you were leaving?
WHILE = two actions at same time · WHEN = one interrupts OR two in sequence

WHEN — нэг үйлдэл нөгөөг нь таслах ЭСВЭЛ дараалсан хоёр үйлдэл

• PS + WHILE + PC = same but other way
• WHEN + PS + PS = two events in sequence

📖  Grammar — Present Perfect Continuous — have/has been + -ing (ongoing action up to now)
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

The PRESENT PERFECT CONTINUOUS (have/has + been + verb-ING) describes an action that STARTED in the past and is STILL CONTINUING now, or has VERY RECENTLY stopped. Focus is on the DURATION of the action. 'I have been studying English for 63 days.' (started 63 days ago, still studying NOW). Compare: Present Perfect Simple = completed result: 'I have studied Chapter 5.' (it's finished). Present Perfect Continuous = ongoing process: 'I have been studying Chapter 5.' (still doing it).

PRESENT PERFECT CONTINUOUS (have/has + been + үйл үг+ING) нь өнгөрсөнд эхэлж ОДОО ХҮРТЭЛ ҮРГЭЛЖИЛЖ байгаа эсвэл МАШ САЯХАН ДУУССАН үйлдлийг тайлбарладаг. Хугацаанд анхаарлаа хандуулдаг. 'I have been studying English for 63 days.' (63 өдрийн өмнө эхэлсэн, ОДОО ч гэсэн суралцаж байна). Харьцуул: Present Perfect Simple = дууссан үр дүн. Present Perfect Continuous = үргэлжилж буй процесс.

📖  Grammar — Present Perfect Continuous for duration — How long have you been...?
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

'HOW LONG HAVE YOU BEEN + -ING?' is the key question for Present Perfect Continuous. It asks about the DURATION of an ongoing action. 'How long have you been learning English?' 'I have been learning for 64 days / since last month.' We use this for: 1) Actions that are STILL HAPPENING: 'She has been teaching here for 10 years.' 2) Actions that JUST STOPPED with a visible result: 'Your eyes are red — have you been crying?' Key: Perfect Continuous emphasises the process and duration, not just the result.

'HOW LONG HAVE YOU BEEN + -ING?' нь Present Perfect Continuous-ийн гол асуулт. Үргэлжилж байсан үйлдлийн ХУГАЦААГ асуудаг. 'How long have you been learning English?' 'I have been learning for 64 days / since last month.' Хэрэглэх тохиолдол: 1) ОДОО Ч ГЭСЭН ҮРГЭЛЖИЛЖ БАЙГАА үйлдэл. 2) САЯХАН ДУУССАН үр дүн харагдаж байгаа үйлдэл: 'Your eyes are red — have you been crying?' Процесс, хугацааг онцолдог.

📖  Grammar — Third Conditional — If I had studied, I would have passed
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

The THIRD CONDITIONAL talks about IMAGINARY situations in the PAST — things that did NOT happen. We use it for REGRETS and SPECULATION about the past. Structure: IF + HAD + past participle, WOULD HAVE + past participle. 'If I HAD STUDIED harder, I WOULD HAVE PASSED the exam.' (I didn't study → I didn't pass → past regret). Compare: 1st Conditional = real future. 2nd Conditional = unreal present. 3rd Conditional = unreal PAST (it already happened — or didn't happen!).

THIRD CONDITIONAL нь ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГ дахь БОДИТ БУС нөхцлийн тухай ярьдаг — болоогүй зүйлс. ХАРАМСАЛ, ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГИЙН ТААМАГЛАЛД хэрэглэнэ. Хэлбэр: IF + HAD + past participle, WOULD HAVE + past participle. 'If I HAD STUDIED, I WOULD HAVE PASSED.' (Суралцаагүй → Тэнцээгүй → өнгөрсөн харамсал). Харьцуул: 1st = бодит ирээдүй. 2nd = бодит бус одоо. 3rd = бодит бус ӨНГӨРСӨН!

📖  Grammar — Mixed Conditionals — combining 2nd and 3rd conditionals
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

MIXED CONDITIONALS combine two different time frames. Type 1 (Past→Present): IF + HAD + past participle (past), WOULD + base verb (present). 'If I HAD STUDIED harder (I didn't — past), I WOULD BE fluent now (I'm not — present).' Type 2 (Present→Past): IF + Past Simple (present), WOULD HAVE + past participle (past). 'If I WERE smarter (I'm not — present), I WOULD HAVE SOLVED that problem (past).' Mixed conditionals show how past actions affect the present and vice versa.

ХОЛИМОГ НӨХЦӨЛТ ӨГҮҮЛБЭР нь хоёр өөр цагийн хүрээг хослуулдаг. 1-р төрөл (Өнгөрсөн→Одоо): IF + HAD + past participle (өнгөрсөн), WOULD + үндсэн үйл үг (одоо). 'If I HAD STUDIED harder, I WOULD BE fluent now.' 2-р төрөл (Одоо→Өнгөрсөн): IF + Past Simple (одоо), WOULD HAVE + past participle (өнгөрсөн). 'If I WERE smarter, I WOULD HAVE SOLVED it.' Холимог нөхцөлт нь өнгөрсөн ба одоогийн хоорондын уялдааг харуулдаг.

Type 2: If I WERE (present) → I WOULD HAVE (past) — одоогийн нөхцөл, өнгөрсөн үр дүн

• Shows links between past and present

📖  Grammar — Must / Might / May — degrees of certainty and probability
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

MODALS OF DEDUCTION express how CERTAIN we are about something. PRESENT deductions: MUST BE = I am almost certain (positive). CAN'T BE = I am almost certain (negative/impossible). MIGHT/MAY/COULD BE = I think it's possible (50% or less). PAST deductions: MUST HAVE + past participle = almost certain about past. CAN'T HAVE + past participle = impossible in the past. MIGHT HAVE + past participle = possible in the past. Scale: must be (95%) > may/might be (50%) > can't be (0%).

ТААМАГЛАЛЫН MODAL ҮЙЛҮГС нь ямар нэгэн зүйлийн тухай ХЭР ИТГЭЛТЭЙ байгааг илэрхийлдэг. ОДООГИЙН таамаглал: MUST BE = бараг тодорхой (эерэг). CAN'T BE = бараг тодорхой (сөрөг). MIGHT/MAY/COULD BE = боломжтой гэж бодно (50% эсвэл бага). ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГИЙН таамаглал: MUST HAVE + p.p. = өнгөрсөнд бараг тодорхой. CAN'T HAVE + p.p. = өнгөрсөнд боломжгүй. MIGHT HAVE + p.p. = өнгөрсөнд боломжтой байсан. Масштаб: must be (95%) > may/might be (50%) > can't be (0%).

📖  Grammar — Might / Could / Should — future predictions with uncertainty
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

For FUTURE UNCERTAINTY, we use modal verbs instead of 'will': MIGHT = small possibility, I'm not very sure (40-60%): 'It might rain.' MAY = slightly more formal, similar to might (40-60%): 'She may arrive late.' COULD = possibility or suggestion (40-60%): 'There could be delays.' SHOULD = expectation, things going as expected (80%): 'The package should arrive tomorrow.' These modals help you sound more natural and less absolute — the future is rarely certain!

ИРЭЭДҮЙН ТОДОРХОЙГҮЙ байдалд 'will'-ийн оронд modal үйлүгс хэрэглэнэ: MIGHT = бага зэрэг боломж, тийм баттай биш (40-60%): 'It might rain.' MAY = нэг хэмжээгээр бүдүүвч might-тэй төстэй (40-60%). COULD = боломж эсвэл санал (40-60%): 'There could be delays.' SHOULD = хүлээлт, зүйл нь зохистой явагдах (80%): 'It should arrive tomorrow.' Эдгээр modal нь ирээдүй нь үргэлж тодорхойгүй гэсэн нарийн утгыг илэрхийлдэг.
Add 'probably' to boost: 'It will probably rain.' (~80%)

FORMAL ENGLISH uses specific vocabulary and structures. Key contrasts: Informal → Formal: 'Hi' → 'Dear Sir/Madam'; 'I want' → 'I would like'; 'Can you?' → 'Could you please?'; 'Thanks' → 'Thank you very much'; 'Sorry' → 'I apologise'; 'Get' → 'obtain/receive'; 'Ask' → 'enquire'; 'About' → 'regarding'. 📖  Grammar — Discourse markers — however, although, despite, nevertheless
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

DISCOURSE MARKERS connect ideas and show logical relationships between sentences. CONTRAST markers: However / Nevertheless / That said (sentence connector + comma). Although / Even though / Whereas (within a sentence). Despite / In spite of (+ noun/-ing, NOT + clause). 'Despite the rain...' (correct) vs 'Despite it rained' (wrong). CAUSE/RESULT: Therefore / Consequently / As a result. CONCESSION: Admittedly / To be fair / On the other hand. These markers transform choppy sentences into flowing, sophisticated B1+ writing.

ХОЛБООС ҮГС нь санааг холбож логик харилцааг харуулдаг. ЭСЭРГҮҮЦЛИЙН холбоос: However / Nevertheless / That said (өгүүлбэр холбогч + таслал). Although / Even though / Whereas (өгүүлбэрийн дотор). Despite / In spite of (+ нэр / -ing, өгүүлбэр биш!). 'Despite the rain...' (зөв) vs 'Despite it rained' (буруу). ШАЛТГААН/ҮР ДҮН: Therefore / Consequently / As a result. БУУЛТ: Admittedly / To be fair / On the other hand.
I agree. However, I have concerns.
Although it's hard, I keep trying.
Despite the rain, we went out.
Despite / In spite of + NOUN or -ING (NOT + clause!)
Despite the rain ✅ · Despite it rained ❌ · Despite raining ✅
However / Nevertheless = start of a NEW sentence (+ comma)
Despite + нэр үг / -ing (өгүүлбэр биш!): Despite the rain / Despite raining
However, Nevertheless — шинэ өгүүлбэрийн эхэнд таслалтай хэрэглэнэ

HOWEVER, what are the benefits? · ALTHOUGH it is difficult, CAN you continue? · DESPITE the challenges, WHAT have you achieved?
• Although / Even though = within a sentence
• Despite / In spite of + noun or -ing (not clause!)
• On the other hand = show another perspective
❌ Despite it rained, we went out.  ✅ Despite THE RAIN, we went out. (despite + noun!)
❌ Although, she passed the test.  ✅ Although she didn't study, she passed. (although needs two clauses!)

❌ In spite of she was nervous, she spoke.  ✅ In spite of BEING nervous, she spoke. (-ing after in spite of!)

📖  Grammar — Wish + Past Simple — I wish I could fly / I wish I had more time
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

WISH + PAST SIMPLE expresses a PRESENT WISH about something that is NOT true or NOT possible NOW. 'I wish I WERE taller.' (I am not tall — present reality.) 'I wish I COULD fly.' (I cannot fly — present reality.) 'I wish I HAD more time.' (I don't have enough time now.) KEY: Always use PAST SIMPLE or COULD (not present simple) after 'wish' for present situations. 'I wish I were' NOT 'I wish I am'. Compare: WISH + WOULD = wishing for a change in someone's behaviour: 'I wish she WOULD stop talking.'

(Би нисч чадахгүй). 'I wish I HAD more time.' (Надад хангалтттай цаг байхгүй). ЧУХАЛ: 'wish'-ийн дараа PAST SIMPLE эсвэл COULD (Present Simple биш!). 'I wish I were' — 'I wish I am' биш. WISH + WOULD = хэн нэгний зан авирыг өөрчлөхийг хүсэх.

📖  Grammar — Indirect questions — Could you tell me where...? / Do you know if...?
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

INDIRECT QUESTIONS are more polite than direct questions. They use statement word order (not question word order). Direct: 'Where IS the hotel?' → Indirect: 'Could you tell me where the hotel IS?' (normal order, no inversion). Direct: 'Is the tour available?' → Indirect: 'Do you know IF the tour is available?' (use if/whether for yes/no questions). Key: NO question mark in indirect questions that use could/would. The sentence ends with a period if it is a polite statement, or ? if it is genuinely a question.

ШУУД БУС АСУУЛТ нь шууд асуултаас илүү зохистой. Тэд өгүүлбэрийн үгийн дарааллыг (асуултын биш) хэрэглэдэг. Шууд: 'Where IS the hotel?' → Шууд бус: 'Could you tell me where the hotel IS?' (хэвийн дараалал, эргүүлэлт байхгүй). Тийм/Үгүй асуултад: 'Do you know IF the tour is available?' (if/whether хэрэглэнэ). ЧУХАЛ: Шууд бус асуулт дахь үгийн дараалал — асуулт биш, өгүүлбэрийн дараалал!
Could you tell me where the hotel is?
Do you know if the tour is free?
I wonder if you could help me.
I'd like to know what time it opens.
Where is the hotel? → where the hotel IS
Where IS the hotel? → Could you tell me where the hotel IS? (verb after subject!)
IS the tour free? → Do you know IF the tour is free? (if + statement order)
ШУУД: Where IS the station? (асуултын дараалал)
ШУУД БУС: Could you tell me where the station IS? (өгүүлбэрийн дараалал)
Тийм/үгүй асуулт: Do you know IF...? / whether...?
Could you tell me where the hotel IS NOT located? · Do you know if there ARE NO tours today? · I wonder if you CAN'T help me?
Could you TELL ME where...? · Do you KNOW if...? · I WONDER whether...? · Would you MIND telling me...?
• Indirect = polite! Use in formal situations.
• Could you tell me what time it IS? (not 'is it'!)
❌ Could you tell me where is the hotel?  ✅ Could you tell me where THE HOTEL IS? (no inversion!)
❌ Do you know if is the tour free?  ✅ Do you know if THE TOUR IS free?
❌ I wonder if can you help me.  ✅ I wonder if YOU CAN help me.
❌ Could you tell me what time does it open?  ✅ Could you tell me what time IT OPENS?
